reason: The actual_json deterministic extraction accurately captures the single table row fragment within the reported bbox, and the annotated page image confirms the extracted cell text (AC-18(2), MONITORING UNAUTHORIZED CONNECTIONS, W: Incorporated into SI-4) matches the visible highlighted row exactly. The expected_json shares the identical bbox, confirming the element scope is this fragment. There is no candidate_corrected_json conflict. Known visual facts explicitly identify this as a documented extractor bounds fragmentation behavior rather than an unresolved structural ambiguity requiring human judgment.

target_evidence_used.fact: Deterministic extraction contains one row with 12 columns, including cell text AC-18(2), MONITORING UNAUTHORIZED CONNECTIONS, and W: Incorporated into SI-4.
Visual evidence shows the highlighted row content matches the extracted cell text exactly.
Expected bbox matches actual_json bbox, confirming the element scope is this single row fragment.
States this is an extractor bounds bug producing row fragments, not a human table/not-table ambiguity.

validators.fact: agent_resolved records do not create human work; human_triage records preserve the explicit question.

recommended_engine_fix: pdf_oxide.extract_tables should merge contiguous row fragments belonging to the same visual table into a single table object rather than emitting isolated single-row tables.
